scope_note: QA Bateson Learning Levels Cert family [191]: formalizes Gregory Bateson's learning hierarchy (0/I/II/III) as a strict invariant filtration on QA state spaces. Four invariants (orbit ⊂ family ⊂ modulus ⊂ ambient category) define five operator classes L_0, L_1, L_2a, L_2b, L_3. Tiered Reachability Theorem: only 26% of S_9 pairs are Level-I reachable; 52.67% require L_2a promotion, 20% require L_2b. Exhaustively verified on S_9 (6561 pairs). Source: Bateson, 'Steps to an Ecology of Mind' (1972); Ashby 'Introduction to Cybernetics' (1956). Checks BLL_1+FILT/TIER/L1/L2A/L2B/L3/STRICT/DB/SRC/WITNESS/F; 1 PASS + 1 FAIL; self-test ok
